title: People's Choice

jokes.text: I pay a cabana boy to wheel me down to the beach every day on a stretcher like a coma patient.
jokes.setup: Asked what he does on vacation in Hawaii
jokes.punchline: Absurdly pathetic image of being wheeled around like an invalid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders neurosis - takes a luxury vacation and makes it sound pathetic and medicalized. Strong character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I pay a cabana boy to wheel me down to the beach every day on a stretcher like a coma patient.

jokes.text: Is that included in the package?
jokes.setup: Larry's absurd vacation description
jokes.punchline: Treating the bizarre scenario as a legitimate travel inquiry
jokes.explanation: Nice straight-man response, economical and well-timed

dialogue.line: Is that included in the package?

jokes.text: It just seems like that's the kind of thing they'd ask me to do, that's all.
jokes.setup: Learning Paul Reiser is hosting People's Choice Awards
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive neediness disguised as casual observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry insecurity - can't directly ask for something so makes it seem like an obvious oversight

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It just seems like that's the kind of thing they'd ask me to do, that's all.

jokes.text: There is a finite amount of videotape in the world.
jokes.setup: Hank wanting to do multiple takes of commercial
jokes.punchline: Treating tape waste as a global resource crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic delivered deadpan. Takes minor annoyance and elevates it to existential crisis level.

dialogue.line: There is a finite amount of videotape in the world.

jokes.text: Some shave creams are coconut-scented, so it's not funny.
jokes.setup: Hank's coconut line in the commercial
jokes.punchline: Explaining why the joke fails with pedantic logic
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about joke construction, typical of show's behind-the-scenes comedy analysis

dialogue.line: Some shave creams are coconut-scented, so it's not funny.

jokes.text: Should I go talk to him? / I wouldn't talk to him at all. / Then you go talk to him.
jokes.setup: Multiple people asking who should talk to upset Hank
jokes.punchline: Circular logic leading to the person who said not to talk going to talk
jokes.explanation: Typical workplace buck-passing elevated to absurd circular logic

dialogue.line: Should I go talk to him?
I wouldn't talk to him at all.
Then you go talk to him.

jokes.text: How could she not be available?
jokes.setup: Being asked if Sally Kirkland is available
jokes.punchline: Implying her career is so dead she must be available
jokes.explanation: Typical Hollywood insider cruelty, economically delivered

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: How could she not be available?

jokes.text: I gave her her first orgasm.
jokes.setup: Defending Sally Kirkland as a 'darling girl'
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate sexual overshare as character reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie character moment - inappropriate sexual bragging as professional recommendation. Economical and shocking.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I gave her her first orgasm.

jokes.text: Oh, well, then she's perfect.
jokes.setup: Artie's inappropriate sexual revelation
jokes.punchline: Treating sexual history as valid professional qualification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry deadpan response to Artie's inappropriate overshare

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, well, then she's perfect.

jokes.text: Maybe he'll do pump it up. That's my favorite song. / I think he'd rather do something off the new album.
jokes.setup: Elvis Costello booked for show
jokes.punchline: Larry's pedestrian music taste vs. artist's creative integrity
jokes.explanation: Classic talk show host disconnect - wants the hit, artist wants to promote new work

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe he'll do pump it up. That's my favorite song.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: I think he'd rather do something off the new album.

jokes.text: So I thought I'd heard that song somewhere before. I had to bring it up.
jokes.setup: Reference to what happened with Michael Bolton
jokes.punchline: Larry accidentally accused artist of plagiarism on air
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry social awkwardness - can't let things go even when inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So I thought I'd heard that song somewhere before. I had to bring it up.

jokes.text: Keep it down because he could be lurking out there. / He does that?
jokes.setup: Discussing Hank's sensitivity about the commercial
jokes.punchline: Treating Hank like a dangerous animal who might be eavesdropping
jokes.explanation: Treating office politics like wildlife management

dialogue.line: Keep it down because he could be lurking out there.
He does that?

jokes.text: Al 'harelip' hartman?
jokes.setup: Being told the regular announcer will do People's Choice
jokes.punchline: Cruel nickname for the announcer
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank cruelty, economical but mean-spirited

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Al 'harelip' hartman?

jokes.text: And he drinks. / So do I. That's why I like him.
jokes.setup: Hank criticizing the announcer for drinking
jokes.punchline: Artie's alcoholism as professional bonding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - turning a criticism into a qualification through shared vice

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And he drinks.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: So do I. That's why I like him.

jokes.text: How many things can he have up his ass at once?
jokes.setup: Multiple complaints about what's bothering Hank
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of metaphor creating absurd logistics question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of common metaphor into absurdist logistics problem

dialogue.line: How many things can he have up his ass at once?

jokes.text: You folks came on a very rare night where you get to see me fuck up 3 jokes in a row. Don't take it for granted.
jokes.setup: Larry bombing with the studio audience
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging failure while making it seem like a special privilege
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on bombing while still trying to salvage the situation - very Larry Sanders

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You folks came on a very rare night where you get to see me fuck up 3 jokes in a row. Don't take it for granted.

jokes.text: What a shit crowd. What a shit, shit crowd.
jokes.setup: Just told audience they're great
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself off-mic with escalating hostility
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry two-facedness - public praise, private hostility

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What a shit crowd. What a shit, shit crowd.

jokes.text: What are they doing, bringing these busloads in from the english-as-a-second-language program?
jokes.setup: Complaining about unresponsive audience
jokes.punchline: Blaming language barrier for his bombing
jokes.explanation: Larry deflecting blame for bombing onto audience demographics

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What are they doing, bringing these busloads in from the english-as-a-second-language program?

jokes.text: I'm only smiling because the audience is looking at me, and I'm a professional.
jokes.setup: Larry appearing to be in good mood on camera
jokes.punchline: Explaining his fake smile as professional obligation
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on showbiz phoniness while demonstrating it

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm only smiling because the audience is looking at me, and I'm a professional.

jokes.text: Thank God. I thought it was something that might have to do with the show we're taping now.
jokes.setup: Told Paul Reiser dropped out of People's Choice
jokes.punchline: Relief that crisis isn't about his current show
jokes.explanation: Larry's self-centered priorities - other people's problems are relief

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Thank God. I thought it was something that might have to do with the show we're taping now.

jokes.text: Andalé, felipe. Vamanos.
jokes.setup: Asking crew member to make quick edit
jokes.punchline: Casual racism disguised as workplace motivation
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Larry's casual insensitivity

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Andalé, felipe. Vamanos.

jokes.text: You'll only have to strip at the end of the show.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about dance numbers
jokes.punchline: Suggesting even more inappropriate alternative
jokes.explanation: Artie's absurd negotiation tactic

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You'll only have to strip at the end of the show.

jokes.text: News travels fast, doesn't it?
jokes.setup: Stevie calling immediately about the People's Choice gig
jokes.punchline: Larry's dry observation about Hollywood's gossip speed
jokes.explanation: Solid industry observation about information flow, though not particularly original or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: News travels fast, doesn't it?

jokes.text: You're cohosting with fucking superman.
jokes.setup: Learning Larry will cohost with Dean Cain
jokes.punchline: Treating superhero role as the actor's actual identity in outrage
jokes.explanation: Stevie's agent hysteria mixed with pop culture confusion

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You're cohosting with fucking superman.

jokes.text: Cohosting detracts from your essential specialness in the marketplace.
jokes.setup: Agent explaining why cohosting is bad
jokes.punchline: Reducing artistic integrity to marketing jargon
jokes.explanation: Perfect agent-speak reducing art to commodity language

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Cohosting detracts from your essential specialness in the marketplace.

jokes.text: Who are you, kathie Lee all of a sudden?
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing to cohost
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to daytime TV cohost
jokes.explanation: Agent putting cohosting in perspective through comparison

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Who are you, kathie Lee all of a sudden?

jokes.text: At the daily planet newspaper in metropolis.
jokes.setup: Asked where Dean Cain works
jokes.punchline: Giving Superman's fictional workplace as real answer
jokes.explanation: Deadpan confusion of fiction and reality

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: At the daily planet newspaper in metropolis.

jokes.text: It's another pleat in your cape.
jokes.setup: Congratulating Dean Cain on People's Choice gig
jokes.punchline: Mixing idiom 'feather in your cap' with Superman costume
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing idiom with superhero costume detail

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It's another pleat in your cape.

jokes.text: You don't wanna be introduced as... superman. Do you?
jokes.setup: Hank pitching himself as announcer
jokes.punchline: Implying current announcer will embarrass Dean Cain with bad pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Hank's desperate sales pitch using manufactured problem

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You don't wanna be introduced as... superman. Do you?

jokes.text: That makes no sense. / Of course it does. Sweetheart, it's the network.
jokes.setup: Larry confused about host vs cohost distinction
jokes.punchline: Network logic as explanation for inexplicable
jokes.explanation: TV industry logic as catch-all explanation for nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Of course it does. Sweetheart, it's the network.

jokes.text: One person is a host. 2 or more are cohosts.
jokes.setup: Confused about hosting arrangement
jokes.punchline: Breaking down simple grammar like profound insight
jokes.explanation: Larry treating basic grammar as revelation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: One person is a host. 2 or more are cohosts.

jokes.text: Rita to bring in the urbanites and the hairdressers
jokes.setup: Explaining demographic targeting for cohosts
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific demographic category
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific demographic targeting

dialogue.line: Rita to bring in the urbanites and the hairdressers

jokes.text: I mean actual hairdressers.
jokes.setup: Larry's confused reaction to hairdresser comment
jokes.punchline: Clarifying the absurdly literal demographic
jokes.explanation: Doubling down on absurd specificity

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I mean actual hairdressers.

jokes.text: Yeah, they are a bunch of dickheads, aren't they?
jokes.setup: Artie blaming network for cohosting decision
jokes.punchline: Larry agreeing just as network executive appears
jokes.explanation: Classic timing comedy - insulting boss just as they appear

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, they are a bunch of dickheads, aren't they?

jokes.text: Well, that doesn't seem that funny, either.
jokes.setup: Larry insisting 'chicken' is funnier than 'coconut'
jokes.punchline: Network executive pointing out neither is particularly funny
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the arbitrary nature of comedy

dialogue.line: Well, that doesn't seem that funny, either.

jokes.text: Chicken is funny in the context of a smell that goes on your face.
jokes.setup: Defending his comedy choice
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining why something should be funny
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperate attempt to intellectualize comedy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Chicken is funny in the context of a smell that goes on your face.

jokes.text: stevie called me up, and he was crying to me, 'why haven't you given Larry this job? Why don't you ask him to host?' Wee wee wee wee wee.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he never asked for the job
jokes.punchline: Artie's mocking impression of crying agent
jokes.explanation: Artie's cruel but accurate impression of agent desperation

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: stevie called me up, and he was crying to me, 'why haven't you given Larry this job? Why don't you ask him to host?' Wee wee wee wee wee.

jokes.text: You should call the conan o'brien people, see if they need a producer for the farewell special.
jokes.setup: Argument about Artie's overcommitment
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Artie work on show about career failure
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to Conan's Tonight Show situation as insult

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You should call the conan o'brien people, see if they need a producer for the farewell special.

jokes.text: No wonder you been married so many times.
jokes.setup: Heated argument with Artie
jokes.punchline: Personal attack connecting work behavior to marriage failures
jokes.explanation: Larry hitting below the belt in argument

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No wonder you been married so many times.

jokes.text: Save your Ivy-league double-talk for your chums down at the parliamentary debating society.
jokes.setup: Phil giving elaborate non-answer about commercial edit
jokes.punchline: Hank's overwrought response to evasive answer
jokes.explanation: Hank's pretentious response to pretentious evasion

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Save your Ivy-league double-talk for your chums down at the parliamentary debating society.

jokes.text: Why is everybody acting like a bunch of 4-year-olds at a fucking barney show?
jokes.setup: Everyone excited about Elvis Costello autographs
jokes.punchline: Comparing grown professionals to children at kids' show
jokes.explanation: Artie's exasperated analogy about professional behavior

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Why is everybody acting like a bunch of 4-year-olds at a fucking barney show?

jokes.text: Is that about a specific person or a pet?
jokes.setup: Asking Elvis Costello about his song 'Allison'
jokes.punchline: Reducing romantic song to question about pets
jokes.explanation: Artie's tone-deaf question to serious artist

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Is that about a specific person or a pet?

jokes.text: I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Being introduced to Elvis Costello
jokes.punchline: Casual rejection of meeting famous musician
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank petulance - rejecting celebrity introduction out of spite

dialogue.line: I don't think so.

jokes.text: Coconut. 2 'k' sounds followed by a nut.
jokes.setup: Still arguing about coconut vs chicken in commercial
jokes.punchline: Breaking down word sounds to prove comedy superiority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Hank's obsessive analysis of comedy theory

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Coconut. 2 'k' sounds followed by a nut.

jokes.text: Mr. Hal linden.
jokes.setup: Suggesting replacement host for People's Choice
jokes.punchline: Offering washed-up 70s sitcom star as threat
jokes.explanation: Artie's underwhelming threat/alternative

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Mr. Hal linden.

jokes.text: Don't gimme any more of that big fan shite.
jokes.setup: Staff claiming to be big fans
jokes.punchline: Musician calling out hollow fan claims
jokes.explanation: Elvis calling out show business phoniness

dialogue.speaker: Elvis Costello
dialogue.line: Don't gimme any more of that big fan shite.

jokes.text: Well, at least he was funny.
jokes.setup: Comparing Larry Sanders Show to Letterman
jokes.punchline: Devastating assessment of Larry's comedy abilities
jokes.explanation: Brutal guest assessment of host's abilities

dialogue.speaker: Elvis Costello
dialogue.line: Well, at least he was funny.

jokes.text: This reminds me of the time angela lansbury was on.
jokes.setup: Looking at Elvis Costello's graffiti on the dressing room wall
jokes.punchline: Comparing Elvis's tantrum to Angela Lansbury's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback suggesting a pattern of guest problems. The Angela Lansbury reference is unexpected and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This reminds me of the time angela lansbury was on.

jokes.text: Rule number one... never quit. Always make them fire you. That way, you get your money.
jokes.setup: Artie explaining he was fired from People's Choice
jokes.punchline: Cynical show business wisdom about getting fired vs quitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie cynicism - turning failure into strategic victory

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Rule number one... never quit. Always make them fire you. That way, you get your money.

jokes.text: I just pulled something. I just... right here.
jokes.setup: Attempting choreographed dance moves for awards show
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury from simple dance step
jokes.explanation: Larry's physical comedy ineptitude

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I just pulled something. I just... right here.

jokes.text: I don't think he dances.
jokes.setup: Larry's obvious discomfort and injury during dance rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Understated assessment of Larry's dance inability
jokes.explanation: Professional dancer's diplomatic assessment

dialogue.speaker: Rita Moreno
dialogue.line: I don't think he dances.

jokes.text: Mmm, tastes a little like coconut.
jokes.setup: Final scene returns to the commercial parody
jokes.punchline: Hank's version finally makes it to air
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending - Hank's version finally airs after all the debate, suggesting his persistence won

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Mmm, tastes a little like coconut.

tmdb_overview: Artie is chosen to produce the People's Choice Awards, prompting both Larry and Hank to try for a role in the show.
